    3.13. CURRENT CI and EC2 Activation Separation GHOST-AWS-CUR-CI-NO-EC2-ACTIVATION A successful ECR push shall not by itself replace the running EC2 container; EC2 activation shall remain a separate manual or boot-triggered action.

    4. 4. CURRENT EC2 Activation and Persistent Data
    • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
      1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
      • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

        This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT
    4.1. CURRENT EC2 Startup Script GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT The current EC2 deployment shall use /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start as the executable activation script.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-FAILFAST
      4.2. CURRENT Startup Failure Semantics GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-FAILFAST The current startup script shall run with set -euo pipefail and shall stop when an unhandled activation command fails.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENT-DIRS
      4.3. CURRENT Persistent Directory Creation GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENT-DIRS The current startup script shall create /home/ubuntu/ragstream_data/doc_raw and /home/ubuntu/ragstream_data/chroma_db before starting the container.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-OPENAI-SSM-READ
      4.4. CURRENT OpenAI Key Retrieval GHOST-AWS-CUR-OPENAI-SSM-READ The current startup script shall read the SecureString parameter /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY from SSM Parameter Store with decryption in eu-central-1 and shall retain the returned value only as runtime process data for container injection.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-SECRET-NOT-IN-IMAGE
        5.3. CURRENT Secret Exclusion from Image GHOST-AWS-CUR-SECRET-NOT-IN-IMAGE The OpenAI API key value and GitHub AWS secret values shall not be copied into the Docker image or stored in version-controlled deployment files.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-ECR-LOGIN-PULL
      4.5. CURRENT ECR Login and Pull GHOST-AWS-CUR-ECR-LOGIN-PULL The current startup script shall obtain a temporary ECR login password, log Docker into the configured registry, and pull the configured latest image before replacing the application container.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-REPLACE
      4.6. CURRENT Streamlit Container Replacement GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-REPLACE If a Docker container named ragstream already exists, the current startup script shall remove it before starting the replacement container.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-RESTART-POLICY
        4.7. CURRENT Streamlit Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-RESTART-POLICY The current Streamlit container shall use Docker restart policy unless-stopped.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-OPENAI-ENV
        4.8. CURRENT OpenAI Environment Injection GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-OPENAI-ENV The current startup script shall inject the SSM-retrieved value into the Streamlit container as OPENAI_API_KEY.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-DOC-RAW-BIND-MOUNT
        4.9. CURRENT Raw-Document Bind Mount GHOST-AWS-CUR-DOC-RAW-BIND-MOUNT The current executed Docker command shall bind /home/ubuntu/ragstream_data/doc_raw to /app/data/doc_raw in the Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-CUR-NO-WHOLE-DATA-MOUNT-CLAIM
          4.11. CURRENT Exact Mount Claim GHOST-AWS-CUR-NO-WHOLE-DATA-MOUNT-CLAIM The current requirement set shall not claim that the executed startup command mounts the entire /home/ubuntu/ragstream_data root to /app/data, because the supplied executable script mounts only the doc_raw and chroma_db subtrees.

          The deployment guide's broader root-mount description shall remain recorded as a source discrepancy in the local profile.
        • GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENCE-SURVIVES-REPLACE
          4.12. CURRENT Persistence Across Container Replacement GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENCE-SURVIVES-REPLACE Files written under /app/data/doc_raw and /app/data/chroma_db shall remain on the EC2/EBS filesystem when the Streamlit container or Docker image is replaced.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-CHROMA-BIND-MOUNT
        4.10. CURRENT Chroma Bind Mount GHOST-AWS-CUR-CHROMA-BIND-MOUNT The current executed Docker command shall bind /home/ubuntu/ragstream_data/chroma_db to /app/data/chroma_db in the Streamlit container.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-IMAGE-PRUNE
      4.13. CURRENT Docker Image Pruning GHOST-AWS-CUR-IMAGE-PRUNE After the container start and Route53 update commands, the current startup script shall invoke Docker image pruning for unused images and shall not fail the activation solely because pruning fails.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-SYSTEMD-UNIT
      4.14. CURRENT systemd Activation Unit GHOST-AWS-CUR-SYSTEMD-UNIT The current EC2 host shall use /etc/systemd/system/ragstream.service as a oneshot unit that executes /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start after network-online.target and docker.service and remains active after the script exits successfully.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-MANUAL-ACTIVATION
        4.15. CURRENT Manual Activation Command GHOST-AWS-CUR-MANUAL-ACTIVATION After a successful image push, the current preferred activation command shall be sudo /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start; rebooting the EC2 instance may alternatively trigger the enabled systemd unit.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT
      4.1. CURRENT EC2 Startup Script GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT The current EC2 deployment shall use /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start as the executable activation script.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    4.2. CURRENT Startup Failure Semantics GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-FAILFAST The current startup script shall run with set -euo pipefail and shall stop when an unhandled activation command fails.

    GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-REPLACE
    4.6. CURRENT Streamlit Container Replacement GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-REPLACE If a Docker container named ragstream already exists, the current startup script shall remove it before starting the replacement container.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-RESTART-POLICY
      4.7. CURRENT Streamlit Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-RESTART-POLICY The current Streamlit container shall use Docker restart policy unless-stopped.

      4.1. CURRENT EC2 Startup Script GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT The current EC2 deployment shall use /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start as the executable activation script.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-IMAGE-PRUNE
    4.13. CURRENT Docker Image Pruning GHOST-AWS-CUR-IMAGE-PRUNE After the container start and Route53 update commands, the current startup script shall invoke Docker image pruning for unused images and shall not fail the activation solely because pruning fails.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT
      4.1. CURRENT EC2 Startup Script GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT The current EC2 deployment shall use /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start as the executable activation script.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-SYSTEMD-UNIT
    4.14. CURRENT systemd Activation Unit GHOST-AWS-CUR-SYSTEMD-UNIT The current EC2 host shall use /etc/systemd/system/ragstream.service as a oneshot unit that executes /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start after network-online.target and docker.service and remains active after the script exits successfully.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-MANUAL-ACTIVATION
      4.15. CURRENT Manual Activation Command GHOST-AWS-CUR-MANUAL-ACTIVATION After a successful image push, the current preferred activation command shall be sudo /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start; rebooting the EC2 instance may alternatively trigger the enabled systemd unit.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-SYSTEMD-UNIT
      4.14. CURRENT systemd Activation Unit GHOST-AWS-CUR-SYSTEMD-UNIT The current EC2 host shall use /etc/systemd/system/ragstream.service as a oneshot unit that executes /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start after network-online.target and docker.service and remains active after the script exits successfully.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT
        4.1. CURRENT EC2 Startup Script GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT The current EC2 deployment shall use /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start as the executable activation script.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-MANUAL-ACTIVATION
    4.15. CURRENT Manual Activation Command GHOST-AWS-CUR-MANUAL-ACTIVATION After a successful image push, the current preferred activation command shall be sudo /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start; rebooting the EC2 instance may alternatively trigger the enabled systemd unit.

    5. 5. CURRENT IAM, Secret Handling, Verification, and Non-Goals
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-HOST
      2.1. CURRENT EC2 Host GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-HOST The current deployment shall run on one Ubuntu 24.04 EC2 instance in a public subnet with a public IPv4 address and an attached IAM instance profile.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-ROLE-PERMISSIONS
    5.1. CURRENT EC2 Role Permissions GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-ROLE-PERMISSIONS The EC2 instance profile shall permit the startup script to pull the configured ECR image, decrypt and read /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY, and change the configured Route53 hosted-zone record.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-NO-EC2-ACCESS-KEY
      5.2. CURRENT No Static AWS Access Key on EC2 GHOST-AWS-CUR-NO-EC2-ACCESS-KEY The EC2 activation path shall use the attached instance role and shall not require an AWS access-key ID or secret access key stored on the instance.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-ROLE-PERMISSIONS
      5.1. CURRENT EC2 Role Permissions GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-ROLE-PERMISSIONS The EC2 instance profile shall permit the startup script to pull the configured ECR image, decrypt and read /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY, and change the configured Route53 hosted-zone record.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-HOST
        2.1. CURRENT EC2 Host GHOST-AWS-CUR-EC2-HOST The current deployment shall run on one Ubuntu 24.04 EC2 instance in a public subnet with a public IPv4 address and an attached IAM instance profile.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.

              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-NO-EC2-ACCESS-KEY
    5.2. CURRENT No Static AWS Access Key on EC2 GHOST-AWS-CUR-NO-EC2-ACCESS-KEY The EC2 activation path shall use the attached instance role and shall not require an AWS access-key ID or secret access key stored on the instance.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-OPENAI-SSM-READ
      4.4. CURRENT OpenAI Key Retrieval GHOST-AWS-CUR-OPENAI-SSM-READ The current startup script shall read the SecureString parameter /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY from SSM Parameter Store with decryption in eu-central-1 and shall retain the returned value only as runtime process data for container injection.
      • GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT
        4.1. CURRENT EC2 Startup Script GHOST-AWS-CUR-START-SCRIPT The current EC2 deployment shall use /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start as the executable activation script.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.

              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-SECRET-NOT-IN-IMAGE
    5.3. CURRENT Secret Exclusion from Image GHOST-AWS-CUR-SECRET-NOT-IN-IMAGE The OpenAI API key value and GitHub AWS secret values shall not be copied into the Docker image or stored in version-controlled deployment files.
    • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
      1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
      • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

        This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK
    5.4. CURRENT Public Endpoint Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com responds through nginx and that users are not required to specify port 8501.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-CHECK
      5.5. CURRENT Container Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that the ragstream container is running with the expected image, restart policy, port publication, two documented bind mounts, and a non-empty OPENAI_API_KEY environment value without printing or otherwise disclosing the secret value.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-NGINX-CERTBOT-CHECK
      5.6. CURRENT nginx and Certificate Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-NGINX-CERTBOT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall include nginx configuration validation, nginx service status, and a Certbot renewal dry run.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-DNS-CHECK
      5.7. CURRENT DNS Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-DNS-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com resolves to the EC2 public IPv4 address written by the startup script.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENCE-CHECK
      5.8. CURRENT Ingestion Persistence Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENCE-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that uploaded raw files appear under the host doc_raw subtree and that Chroma data and file_manifest.json appear under the host chroma_db project subtree.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK
      5.4. CURRENT Public Endpoint Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com responds through nginx and that users are not required to specify port 8501.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.

    GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-CHECK
    5.5. CURRENT Container Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-CONTAINER-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that the ragstream container is running with the expected image, restart policy, port publication, two documented bind mounts, and a non-empty OPENAI_API_KEY environment value without printing or otherwise disclosing the secret value.
    • GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK
      5.4. CURRENT Public Endpoint Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com responds through nginx and that users are not required to specify port 8501.

    5.6. CURRENT nginx and Certificate Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-NGINX-CERTBOT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall include nginx configuration validation, nginx service status, and a Certbot renewal dry run.

      5.4. CURRENT Public Endpoint Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com responds through nginx and that users are not required to specify port 8501.

        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.

      5.4. CURRENT Public Endpoint Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PUBLIC-ENDPOINT-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com responds through nginx and that users are not required to specify port 8501.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.

    GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENCE-CHECK
    5.8. CURRENT Ingestion Persistence Verification GHOST-AWS-CUR-PERSISTENCE-CHECK Current deployment verification shall confirm that uploaded raw files appear under the host doc_raw subtree and that Chroma data and file_manifest.json appear under the host chroma_db project subtree.
    • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
      1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
      • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

        This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
