Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-CUR-PHASE1-NONGOALS
    5.9. CURRENT Phase-1 Non-Goals GHOST-AWS-CUR-PHASE1-NONGOALS The current AWS deployment shall not be represented as providing Cognito-triggered instance start, an Application Load Balancer, CloudFront as an always-on front door, autoscaling, multi-instance operation, multi-tenant hardening, or public backend ports other than 80 and 443.

    6. 6. TARGET MCP Hosting and Network Topology
    • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
      1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
      • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

        This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
    6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
      6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND
        6.5. TARGET MCP Loopback Host Binding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND The MCP container port 8000 shall be published only as 127.0.0.1:8000 on the EC2 host and shall not be bound to the EC2 public interfaces.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000
          6.6. TARGET No Public MCP Backend Port GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000 The EC2 Security Group shall contain no inbound rule for TCP port 8000.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP
          6.8. TARGET Local Proxy Hop GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP The nginx-to-MCP backend hop shall use HTTP only over EC2 loopback at http://127.0.0.1:8000 because public TLS terminates at nginx.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
        6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE
          6.13. TARGET MCP Activation Sequence GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE The independent MCP activation mechanism shall authenticate Docker to ECR through the EC2 instance role, pull the recorded MCP image, remove only a pre-existing MCP container with the recorded container name, and start the replacement MCP container with the approved loopback port publication and runtime configuration.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY
            6.14. TARGET MCP Container Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY The MCP container shall use the Docker restart policy unless-stopped, matching the current Streamlit container's restart behavior.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION
          6.15. TARGET MCP Boot Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION The EC2 host shall use a systemd unit separate from ragstream.service to invoke the independent MCP activation mechanism after network-online.target and docker.service during instance boot.

          The exact unit and activation paths shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before installation.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SSM-INJECTION
          6.16. TARGET MCP Runtime Secret Injection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SSM-INJECTION The MCP activation mechanism shall read the existing OpenAI API key and every secret required by the selected OAuth realization from recorded SSM Parameter Store references or from the selected external authorization provider at runtime.

          Secret values shall not be embedded in the image, activation artifact, systemd unit, nginx configuration, repository, or local deployment profile.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EC2-ROLE
            6.17. TARGET MCP EC2 Role Permissions GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EC2-ROLE The existing EC2 instance role shall be extended only as required to pull the recorded MCP image and read the exact SSM parameters used by the MCP activation mechanism.

            The MCP deployment shall not require a static AWS access-key ID or secret access key on EC2.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-IMAGE-RECORD
        6.10. TARGET MCP Image Reference Recording GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-IMAGE-RECORD The exact image reference used by the MCP container shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before deployment.

        This requirement shall not predetermine whether the MCP implementation uses the current application image or a separate image.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS
        6.11. TARGET No Project or Memory Data Mounts GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS The initial MCP container shall not mount the current doc_raw, chroma_db, memory, or other project-persistence directories because the approved initial ghost_engineer_prompt tool requires only prompt-processing code and configuration.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE
          10.8. TARGET MCP Scope Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE MCP acceptance shall verify that no document, Chroma, memory, GUI, or other project data mount is attached to the initial MCP container and that no additional GHOST tool is advertised.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ECR-DELIVERY
        6.12. TARGET Controlled MCP Image Delivery GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ECR-DELIVERY The MCP runtime image shall be built by GitHub Actions and pushed to a private Amazon ECR repository through the same controlled deployment gate used by the current deployment: manual workflow dispatch or an eligible main-branch push whose head commit message contains [deploy].

        The exact Dockerfile, workflow path, ECR repository, and image reference shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before deployment.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-DEPENDENCY
        9.5. TARGET MCP Runtime Dependency Declaration GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-DEPENDENCY The MCP server library and exact deployed version shall be declared in the build dependency set and recorded in the local deployment profile before the MCP image is built and accepted.

        The requirement set shall not prescribe an unverified library version.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-VALUES
        10.1. TARGET MCP Runtime Value Recording GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-VALUES Before deployment, the local profile shall record the exact MCP image reference, container name, container entry command, MCP server library and version, activation artifact paths, authorization-server issuer, authorization metadata URL, required scopes, accepted client origins, and client registration values actually used.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
      6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE
        6.4. TARGET Exact nginx MCP Route GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE The Certbot-managed HTTPS server block for ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall contain an exact /mcp route that proxies to the local MCP backend without changing the existing Streamlit location / route.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP
          7.1. TARGET Streamable HTTP Transport GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP The MCP server shall implement the MCP Streamable HTTP transport at the single /mcp endpoint.

          HTTP POST shall process MCP JSON-RPC messages. HTTP GET shall either open the transport's server-to-client event stream or return HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed when the server does not offer a standalone event stream.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-JSONRPC-UTF8
            7.2. TARGET MCP JSON-RPC Encoding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-JSONRPC-UTF8 MCP protocol messages shall use JSON-RPC and UTF-8 encoding in accordance with the negotiated MCP protocol version.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-HANDSHAKE
            7.3. TARGET MCP Initialization Handshake GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-HANDSHAKE The endpoint shall complete initialize and notifications/initialized before accepting normal tool discovery and invocation for a client session.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOOL-PROTOCOL
            7.4. TARGET MCP Tool Operations GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOOL-PROTOCOL The endpoint shall support tools/list and tools/call for the single ghost_engineer_prompt tool defined by 73_mcp_interface.sdoc.

            The endpoint shall not advertise a standalone PreProcessing tool, a standalone A2 tool, or any additional GHOST capability under the initial requirement set.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMPT-ENGINEERING-TOOL
              9.1. TARGET Single Prompt-Engineering MCP Tool GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMPT-ENGINEERING-TOOL The MCP server shall deploy exactly the single ghost_engineer_prompt tool defined by 73_mcp_interface.sdoc.

              For each accepted invocation, the adapter shall create a fresh request-isolated SuperPrompt, invoke the existing deterministic preprocess(...) prompt-construction function, then invoke the existing A2PromptShaper LLM path on the same SuperPrompt, and return the post-A2 engineered prompt through the MCP result contract.

              The adapter shall not invoke AppController.preprocess(), ActiveBrief discovery, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval, memory, or any other GHOST pipeline stage.

              Deterministic PreProcessing and A2 shall remain internal sequential stages of this one tool and shall not be advertised as separate MCP tools.
              • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OPENAI-KEY
                9.2. TARGET MCP OpenAI Key Injection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OPENAI-KEY The MCP container shall receive the existing OpenAI API key from /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY at runtime for the A2 execution inside ghost_engineer_prompt.

                The deterministic prompt-construction step preceding A2 shall not call an LLM.
              • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CONFIG-PACKAGING
                9.3. TARGET MCP Prompt Configuration Packaging GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CONFIG-PACKAGING The MCP runtime image shall contain the existing prompt schema, A2 agent configuration, option catalogs, and Python modules required by the single approved ghost_engineer_prompt execution path.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-EXTRA-TOOLS
              9.4. TARGET No Additional MCP Tools GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-EXTRA-TOOLS The initial tools/list result shall contain exactly ghost_engineer_prompt and shall contain no standalone PreProcessing tool, standalone A2 tool, or additional GHOST tool.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION
            7.5. TARGET Origin Validation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION The MCP HTTP server shall validate every present Origin header against an explicitly configured allowlist and shall return HTTP 403 when a present Origin is not allowed.

            Client-origin values shall be recorded in the local profile only after they are established by provider documentation or real acceptance testing.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
            10.7. TARGET MCP Protocol Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP protocol acceptance shall complete initialize, notifications/initialized, tools/list, and one successful tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt through the public HTTPS URL.

            The returned result shall contain the engineered prompt produced only after the internal deterministic prompt-construction step and A2 execution have completed.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-HEADERS
          7.6. TARGET MCP Proxy Headers GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-HEADERS The nginx /mcp route shall preserve Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto, Authorization, Origin, Content-Type, Accept, MCP-Protocol-Version, MCP-Session-Id, and Last-Event-ID information required by the backend to process authenticated MCP requests correctly.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-STREAMING
          7.7. TARGET MCP Proxy Streaming Behavior GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-STREAMING The nginx /mcp route shall disable response buffering and caching and shall use proxy timeouts that permit the Streamable HTTP response mode used by the MCP server.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION
          10.2. TARGET Current Streamlit Route Regression Check GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION After MCP deployment, https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall continue to serve the current Streamlit application through the existing nginx root route.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS
        6.7. TARGET External HTTPS Only GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS Claude and ChatGPT shall reach the MCP service only over HTTPS port 443 through nginx; they shall never connect directly to port 8000 or to an unencrypted public MCP URL.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-DNS-REUSE
        6.18. TARGET Existing DNS Record Reuse GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-DNS-REUSE The MCP endpoint shall reuse the existing ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com Route53 A record and shall not require a second public DNS record because the MCP route is hosted under the existing application hostname.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CERTIFICATE-REUSE
        6.19. TARGET Existing TLS Certificate Reuse GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CERTIFICATE-REUSE The MCP endpoint shall use the existing Certbot-managed TLS certificate for ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com through the existing nginx HTTPS server block and shall not terminate public TLS inside the MCP container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE
        8.1. TARGET OAuth-Protected MCP Resource GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE The MCP endpoint shall act as an OAuth-protected resource server for HTTP transport and shall require a valid access token before processing tool discovery or tool invocation.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-METADATA
          8.2. TARGET Protected Resource Metadata GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-METADATA The deployment shall publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata at https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp for the canonical MCP resource https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp and shall identify at least one authorization server used by the clients.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SERVER-DISCOVERY
            8.3. TARGET Authorization-Server Discovery GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SERVER-DISCOVERY The selected authorization server shall publish OAuth Authorization Server Metadata or OpenID Connect discovery metadata containing the endpoints and capabilities required by the MCP clients.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PKCE-S256
              8.8. TARGET OAuth PKCE S256 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PKCE-S256 The authorization-code flow used by MCP clients shall support PKCE with the S256 code-challenge method and shall advertise that capability in authorization-server metadata.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-HTTPS
              8.9. TARGET OAuth HTTPS Boundary GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-HTTPS Authorization-server endpoints and non-localhost redirect URIs used by the remote MCP connection shall use HTTPS.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-REFRESH-CONNECTION
              8.10. TARGET Refreshable Client Connection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-REFRESH-CONNECTION The authorization configuration shall issue and accept refresh tokens in the manner required by the selected Claude and ChatGPT client flows so an authorized connection can remain usable after the initial access token expires.
              • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OFFLINE-ACCESS-METADATA
                8.11. TARGET ChatGPT Refresh Metadata GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OFFLINE-ACCESS-METADATA When the selected authorization provider uses offline_access to request refresh tokens, its discovery metadata shall advertise offline_access and the ChatGPT connection shall request it.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLIENT-REGISTRATION
              8.12. TARGET Client Registration Compatibility GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLIENT-REGISTRATION The selected client-registration mechanism shall be one supported by the configured Claude connector and one accepted by the configured ChatGPT custom app.

              The exact mechanism, client identifiers, client-secret use, and redirect URIs shall be recorded in the local profile only after client configuration or acceptance testing establishes them.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-401-CHALLENGE
            8.4. TARGET Unauthenticated MCP Challenge GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-401-CHALLENGE An unauthenticated request to /mcp shall return HTTP 401 and shall provide the protected-resource metadata location through the standards-defined discovery mechanism used by the MCP client.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-DISCOVERY-ACCEPTANCE
            10.4. TARGET OAuth Discovery Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-DISCOVERY-ACCEPTANCE MCP deployment acceptance shall confirm that an unauthenticated /mcp request receives the required authorization challenge and that protected-resource and authorization-server discovery documents resolve to the configured canonical values.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-BEARER-HEADER
          8.5. TARGET Bearer-Token Header GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-BEARER-HEADER Authenticated MCP requests shall carry the access token in the HTTP Authorization Bearer header, and the deployment shall not accept access tokens in the request URI query string.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-INDICATOR
          8.6. TARGET MCP Resource Indicator GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-INDICATOR The OAuth authorization and token flow shall bind issued access tokens to the canonical MCP resource identifier so the MCP server can validate that each token was issued for this endpoint.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOKEN-VALIDATION
          8.7. TARGET MCP Access-Token Validation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOKEN-VALIDATION The MCP server shall reject missing, expired, invalid, wrongly scoped, or wrong-audience access tokens before executing a tool.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SECRETS
          8.13. TARGET OAuth Secret Storage GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SECRETS OAuth client secrets, signing or validation secrets, and refresh-token storage credentials required by the selected authorization implementation shall be held in the selected AWS or authorization-provider secret store and shall not be hard-coded into the repository, Dockerfile, image, nginx configuration, or local profile.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-TOKEN-PASSTHROUGH
          8.14. TARGET No MCP Token Passthrough GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-TOKEN-PASSTHROUGH The MCP access token received from Claude or ChatGPT shall be used only to authorize the MCP request and shall not be forwarded to OpenAI or any other downstream API.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE
          10.5. TARGET Claude Client Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE The target shall not be promoted to CURRENT until Claude can add the remote MCP server, complete OAuth authorization, discover ghost_engineer_prompt as the sole GHOST tool, invoke it successfully, receive the post-A2 engineered prompt, and continue after an access-token refresh.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE
          10.6. TARGET ChatGPT Client Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE The target shall not be promoted to CURRENT until the owner's ChatGPT web account can create and enable the custom MCP plugin, complete OAuth authorization, discover ghost_engineer_prompt as the sole GHOST tool, invoke it successfully, receive the post-A2 engineered prompt in the same conversation, and continue after an access-token refresh.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NETWORK-ACCEPTANCE
        10.3. TARGET MCP Network Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NETWORK-ACCEPTANCE MCP deployment acceptance shall confirm that /mcp is reachable over HTTPS port 443, that host port 8000 listens only on 127.0.0.1, and that the Security Group has no inbound rule for port 8000.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMOTION-GATE
      10.9. TARGET MCP Status Promotion Gate GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMOTION-GATE The MCP requirements shall remain TARGET until the implementation exists on the EC2 instance and all network, OAuth discovery, Claude, ChatGPT, protocol, tool-scope, and Streamlit-regression acceptance requirements pass.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
      6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
    6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND
      6.5. TARGET MCP Loopback Host Binding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND The MCP container port 8000 shall be published only as 127.0.0.1:8000 on the EC2 host and shall not be bound to the EC2 public interfaces.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000
        6.6. TARGET No Public MCP Backend Port GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000 The EC2 Security Group shall contain no inbound rule for TCP port 8000.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP
        6.8. TARGET Local Proxy Hop GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP The nginx-to-MCP backend hop shall use HTTP only over EC2 loopback at http://127.0.0.1:8000 because public TLS terminates at nginx.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
      6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE
        6.13. TARGET MCP Activation Sequence GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE The independent MCP activation mechanism shall authenticate Docker to ECR through the EC2 instance role, pull the recorded MCP image, remove only a pre-existing MCP container with the recorded container name, and start the replacement MCP container with the approved loopback port publication and runtime configuration.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY
          6.14. TARGET MCP Container Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY The MCP container shall use the Docker restart policy unless-stopped, matching the current Streamlit container's restart behavior.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION
        6.15. TARGET MCP Boot Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION The EC2 host shall use a systemd unit separate from ragstream.service to invoke the independent MCP activation mechanism after network-online.target and docker.service during instance boot.

        The exact unit and activation paths shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before installation.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SSM-INJECTION
        6.16. TARGET MCP Runtime Secret Injection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SSM-INJECTION The MCP activation mechanism shall read the existing OpenAI API key and every secret required by the selected OAuth realization from recorded SSM Parameter Store references or from the selected external authorization provider at runtime.

        Secret values shall not be embedded in the image, activation artifact, systemd unit, nginx configuration, repository, or local deployment profile.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EC2-ROLE
          6.17. TARGET MCP EC2 Role Permissions GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EC2-ROLE The existing EC2 instance role shall be extended only as required to pull the recorded MCP image and read the exact SSM parameters used by the MCP activation mechanism.

          The MCP deployment shall not require a static AWS access-key ID or secret access key on EC2.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-IMAGE-RECORD
      6.10. TARGET MCP Image Reference Recording GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-IMAGE-RECORD The exact image reference used by the MCP container shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before deployment.

      This requirement shall not predetermine whether the MCP implementation uses the current application image or a separate image.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS
      6.11. TARGET No Project or Memory Data Mounts GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS The initial MCP container shall not mount the current doc_raw, chroma_db, memory, or other project-persistence directories because the approved initial ghost_engineer_prompt tool requires only prompt-processing code and configuration.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE
        10.8. TARGET MCP Scope Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE MCP acceptance shall verify that no document, Chroma, memory, GUI, or other project data mount is attached to the initial MCP container and that no additional GHOST tool is advertised.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ECR-DELIVERY
      6.12. TARGET Controlled MCP Image Delivery GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ECR-DELIVERY The MCP runtime image shall be built by GitHub Actions and pushed to a private Amazon ECR repository through the same controlled deployment gate used by the current deployment: manual workflow dispatch or an eligible main-branch push whose head commit message contains [deploy].

      The exact Dockerfile, workflow path, ECR repository, and image reference shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before deployment.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-DEPENDENCY
      9.5. TARGET MCP Runtime Dependency Declaration GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-DEPENDENCY The MCP server library and exact deployed version shall be declared in the build dependency set and recorded in the local deployment profile before the MCP image is built and accepted.

      The requirement set shall not prescribe an unverified library version.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-VALUES
      10.1. TARGET MCP Runtime Value Recording GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RUNTIME-VALUES Before deployment, the local profile shall record the exact MCP image reference, container name, container entry command, MCP server library and version, activation artifact paths, authorization-server issuer, authorization metadata URL, required scopes, accepted client origins, and client registration values actually used.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
      6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
    6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE
      6.4. TARGET Exact nginx MCP Route GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE The Certbot-managed HTTPS server block for ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall contain an exact /mcp route that proxies to the local MCP backend without changing the existing Streamlit location / route.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP
        7.1. TARGET Streamable HTTP Transport GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP The MCP server shall implement the MCP Streamable HTTP transport at the single /mcp endpoint.

        HTTP POST shall process MCP JSON-RPC messages. HTTP GET shall either open the transport's server-to-client event stream or return HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed when the server does not offer a standalone event stream.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-JSONRPC-UTF8
          7.2. TARGET MCP JSON-RPC Encoding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-JSONRPC-UTF8 MCP protocol messages shall use JSON-RPC and UTF-8 encoding in accordance with the negotiated MCP protocol version.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-HANDSHAKE
          7.3. TARGET MCP Initialization Handshake GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-HANDSHAKE The endpoint shall complete initialize and notifications/initialized before accepting normal tool discovery and invocation for a client session.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOOL-PROTOCOL
          7.4. TARGET MCP Tool Operations GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOOL-PROTOCOL The endpoint shall support tools/list and tools/call for the single ghost_engineer_prompt tool defined by 73_mcp_interface.sdoc.

          The endpoint shall not advertise a standalone PreProcessing tool, a standalone A2 tool, or any additional GHOST capability under the initial requirement set.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMPT-ENGINEERING-TOOL
            9.1. TARGET Single Prompt-Engineering MCP Tool GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMPT-ENGINEERING-TOOL The MCP server shall deploy exactly the single ghost_engineer_prompt tool defined by 73_mcp_interface.sdoc.

            For each accepted invocation, the adapter shall create a fresh request-isolated SuperPrompt, invoke the existing deterministic preprocess(...) prompt-construction function, then invoke the existing A2PromptShaper LLM path on the same SuperPrompt, and return the post-A2 engineered prompt through the MCP result contract.

            The adapter shall not invoke AppController.preprocess(), ActiveBrief discovery, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval, memory, or any other GHOST pipeline stage.

            Deterministic PreProcessing and A2 shall remain internal sequential stages of this one tool and shall not be advertised as separate MCP tools.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OPENAI-KEY
              9.2. TARGET MCP OpenAI Key Injection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OPENAI-KEY The MCP container shall receive the existing OpenAI API key from /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY at runtime for the A2 execution inside ghost_engineer_prompt.

              The deterministic prompt-construction step preceding A2 shall not call an LLM.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CONFIG-PACKAGING
              9.3. TARGET MCP Prompt Configuration Packaging GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CONFIG-PACKAGING The MCP runtime image shall contain the existing prompt schema, A2 agent configuration, option catalogs, and Python modules required by the single approved ghost_engineer_prompt execution path.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-EXTRA-TOOLS
            9.4. TARGET No Additional MCP Tools GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-EXTRA-TOOLS The initial tools/list result shall contain exactly ghost_engineer_prompt and shall contain no standalone PreProcessing tool, standalone A2 tool, or additional GHOST tool.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION
          7.5. TARGET Origin Validation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION The MCP HTTP server shall validate every present Origin header against an explicitly configured allowlist and shall return HTTP 403 when a present Origin is not allowed.

          Client-origin values shall be recorded in the local profile only after they are established by provider documentation or real acceptance testing.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
          10.7. TARGET MCP Protocol Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP protocol acceptance shall complete initialize, notifications/initialized, tools/list, and one successful tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt through the public HTTPS URL.

          The returned result shall contain the engineered prompt produced only after the internal deterministic prompt-construction step and A2 execution have completed.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-HEADERS
        7.6. TARGET MCP Proxy Headers GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-HEADERS The nginx /mcp route shall preserve Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto, Authorization, Origin, Content-Type, Accept, MCP-Protocol-Version, MCP-Session-Id, and Last-Event-ID information required by the backend to process authenticated MCP requests correctly.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-STREAMING
        7.7. TARGET MCP Proxy Streaming Behavior GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-STREAMING The nginx /mcp route shall disable response buffering and caching and shall use proxy timeouts that permit the Streamable HTTP response mode used by the MCP server.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION
        10.2. TARGET Current Streamlit Route Regression Check GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION After MCP deployment, https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall continue to serve the current Streamlit application through the existing nginx root route.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS
      6.7. TARGET External HTTPS Only GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS Claude and ChatGPT shall reach the MCP service only over HTTPS port 443 through nginx; they shall never connect directly to port 8000 or to an unencrypted public MCP URL.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-DNS-REUSE
      6.18. TARGET Existing DNS Record Reuse GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-DNS-REUSE The MCP endpoint shall reuse the existing ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com Route53 A record and shall not require a second public DNS record because the MCP route is hosted under the existing application hostname.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CERTIFICATE-REUSE
      6.19. TARGET Existing TLS Certificate Reuse GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CERTIFICATE-REUSE The MCP endpoint shall use the existing Certbot-managed TLS certificate for ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com through the existing nginx HTTPS server block and shall not terminate public TLS inside the MCP container.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE
      8.1. TARGET OAuth-Protected MCP Resource GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE The MCP endpoint shall act as an OAuth-protected resource server for HTTP transport and shall require a valid access token before processing tool discovery or tool invocation.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-METADATA
        8.2. TARGET Protected Resource Metadata GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-METADATA The deployment shall publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata at https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp for the canonical MCP resource https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp and shall identify at least one authorization server used by the clients.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SERVER-DISCOVERY
          8.3. TARGET Authorization-Server Discovery GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SERVER-DISCOVERY The selected authorization server shall publish OAuth Authorization Server Metadata or OpenID Connect discovery metadata containing the endpoints and capabilities required by the MCP clients.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PKCE-S256
            8.8. TARGET OAuth PKCE S256 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PKCE-S256 The authorization-code flow used by MCP clients shall support PKCE with the S256 code-challenge method and shall advertise that capability in authorization-server metadata.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-HTTPS
            8.9. TARGET OAuth HTTPS Boundary GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-HTTPS Authorization-server endpoints and non-localhost redirect URIs used by the remote MCP connection shall use HTTPS.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-REFRESH-CONNECTION
            8.10. TARGET Refreshable Client Connection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-REFRESH-CONNECTION The authorization configuration shall issue and accept refresh tokens in the manner required by the selected Claude and ChatGPT client flows so an authorized connection can remain usable after the initial access token expires.
            • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OFFLINE-ACCESS-METADATA
              8.11. TARGET ChatGPT Refresh Metadata GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OFFLINE-ACCESS-METADATA When the selected authorization provider uses offline_access to request refresh tokens, its discovery metadata shall advertise offline_access and the ChatGPT connection shall request it.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLIENT-REGISTRATION
            8.12. TARGET Client Registration Compatibility GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLIENT-REGISTRATION The selected client-registration mechanism shall be one supported by the configured Claude connector and one accepted by the configured ChatGPT custom app.

            The exact mechanism, client identifiers, client-secret use, and redirect URIs shall be recorded in the local profile only after client configuration or acceptance testing establishes them.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-401-CHALLENGE
          8.4. TARGET Unauthenticated MCP Challenge GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-401-CHALLENGE An unauthenticated request to /mcp shall return HTTP 401 and shall provide the protected-resource metadata location through the standards-defined discovery mechanism used by the MCP client.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-DISCOVERY-ACCEPTANCE
          10.4. TARGET OAuth Discovery Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-DISCOVERY-ACCEPTANCE MCP deployment acceptance shall confirm that an unauthenticated /mcp request receives the required authorization challenge and that protected-resource and authorization-server discovery documents resolve to the configured canonical values.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-BEARER-HEADER
        8.5. TARGET Bearer-Token Header GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-BEARER-HEADER Authenticated MCP requests shall carry the access token in the HTTP Authorization Bearer header, and the deployment shall not accept access tokens in the request URI query string.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-INDICATOR
        8.6. TARGET MCP Resource Indicator GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-INDICATOR The OAuth authorization and token flow shall bind issued access tokens to the canonical MCP resource identifier so the MCP server can validate that each token was issued for this endpoint.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOKEN-VALIDATION
        8.7. TARGET MCP Access-Token Validation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOKEN-VALIDATION The MCP server shall reject missing, expired, invalid, wrongly scoped, or wrong-audience access tokens before executing a tool.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SECRETS
        8.13. TARGET OAuth Secret Storage GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-SECRETS OAuth client secrets, signing or validation secrets, and refresh-token storage credentials required by the selected authorization implementation shall be held in the selected AWS or authorization-provider secret store and shall not be hard-coded into the repository, Dockerfile, image, nginx configuration, or local profile.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-TOKEN-PASSTHROUGH
        8.14. TARGET No MCP Token Passthrough GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-TOKEN-PASSTHROUGH The MCP access token received from Claude or ChatGPT shall be used only to authorize the MCP request and shall not be forwarded to OpenAI or any other downstream API.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE
        10.5. TARGET Claude Client Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE The target shall not be promoted to CURRENT until Claude can add the remote MCP server, complete OAuth authorization, discover ghost_engineer_prompt as the sole GHOST tool, invoke it successfully, receive the post-A2 engineered prompt, and continue after an access-token refresh.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE
        10.6. TARGET ChatGPT Client Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE The target shall not be promoted to CURRENT until the owner's ChatGPT web account can create and enable the custom MCP plugin, complete OAuth authorization, discover ghost_engineer_prompt as the sole GHOST tool, invoke it successfully, receive the post-A2 engineered prompt in the same conversation, and continue after an access-token refresh.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NETWORK-ACCEPTANCE
      10.3. TARGET MCP Network Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NETWORK-ACCEPTANCE MCP deployment acceptance shall confirm that /mcp is reachable over HTTPS port 443, that host port 8000 listens only on 127.0.0.1, and that the Security Group has no inbound rule for port 8000.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
      6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE
    6.4. TARGET Exact nginx MCP Route GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE The Certbot-managed HTTPS server block for ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall contain an exact /mcp route that proxies to the local MCP backend without changing the existing Streamlit location / route.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      7.1. TARGET Streamable HTTP Transport GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP The MCP server shall implement the MCP Streamable HTTP transport at the single /mcp endpoint.

      HTTP POST shall process MCP JSON-RPC messages. HTTP GET shall either open the transport's server-to-client event stream or return HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed when the server does not offer a standalone event stream.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-JSONRPC-UTF8
        7.2. TARGET MCP JSON-RPC Encoding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-JSONRPC-UTF8 MCP protocol messages shall use JSON-RPC and UTF-8 encoding in accordance with the negotiated MCP protocol version.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-HANDSHAKE
        7.3. TARGET MCP Initialization Handshake GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-HANDSHAKE The endpoint shall complete initialize and notifications/initialized before accepting normal tool discovery and invocation for a client session.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOOL-PROTOCOL
        7.4. TARGET MCP Tool Operations GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-TOOL-PROTOCOL The endpoint shall support tools/list and tools/call for the single ghost_engineer_prompt tool defined by 73_mcp_interface.sdoc.

        The endpoint shall not advertise a standalone PreProcessing tool, a standalone A2 tool, or any additional GHOST capability under the initial requirement set.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMPT-ENGINEERING-TOOL
          9.1. TARGET Single Prompt-Engineering MCP Tool GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMPT-ENGINEERING-TOOL The MCP server shall deploy exactly the single ghost_engineer_prompt tool defined by 73_mcp_interface.sdoc.

          For each accepted invocation, the adapter shall create a fresh request-isolated SuperPrompt, invoke the existing deterministic preprocess(...) prompt-construction function, then invoke the existing A2PromptShaper LLM path on the same SuperPrompt, and return the post-A2 engineered prompt through the MCP result contract.

          The adapter shall not invoke AppController.preprocess(), ActiveBrief discovery, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval, memory, or any other GHOST pipeline stage.

          Deterministic PreProcessing and A2 shall remain internal sequential stages of this one tool and shall not be advertised as separate MCP tools.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OPENAI-KEY
            9.2. TARGET MCP OpenAI Key Injection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OPENAI-KEY The MCP container shall receive the existing OpenAI API key from /ragstream/prod/OPENAI_API_KEY at runtime for the A2 execution inside ghost_engineer_prompt.

            The deterministic prompt-construction step preceding A2 shall not call an LLM.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CONFIG-PACKAGING
            9.3. TARGET MCP Prompt Configuration Packaging GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CONFIG-PACKAGING The MCP runtime image shall contain the existing prompt schema, A2 agent configuration, option catalogs, and Python modules required by the single approved ghost_engineer_prompt execution path.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-EXTRA-TOOLS
          9.4. TARGET No Additional MCP Tools GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-EXTRA-TOOLS The initial tools/list result shall contain exactly ghost_engineer_prompt and shall contain no standalone PreProcessing tool, standalone A2 tool, or additional GHOST tool.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION
        7.5. TARGET Origin Validation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ORIGIN-VALIDATION The MCP HTTP server shall validate every present Origin header against an explicitly configured allowlist and shall return HTTP 403 when a present Origin is not allowed.

        Client-origin values shall be recorded in the local profile only after they are established by provider documentation or real acceptance testing.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
        10.7. TARGET MCP Protocol Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP protocol acceptance shall complete initialize, notifications/initialized, tools/list, and one successful tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt through the public HTTPS URL.

        The returned result shall contain the engineered prompt produced only after the internal deterministic prompt-construction step and A2 execution have completed.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-HEADERS
      7.6. TARGET MCP Proxy Headers GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-HEADERS The nginx /mcp route shall preserve Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto, Authorization, Origin, Content-Type, Accept, MCP-Protocol-Version, MCP-Session-Id, and Last-Event-ID information required by the backend to process authenticated MCP requests correctly.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-STREAMING
      7.7. TARGET MCP Proxy Streaming Behavior GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROXY-STREAMING The nginx /mcp route shall disable response buffering and caching and shall use proxy timeouts that permit the Streamable HTTP response mode used by the MCP server.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION
      10.2. TARGET Current Streamlit Route Regression Check GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION After MCP deployment, https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall continue to serve the current Streamlit application through the existing nginx root route.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
      6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND
    6.5. TARGET MCP Loopback Host Binding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND The MCP container port 8000 shall be published only as 127.0.0.1:8000 on the EC2 host and shall not be bound to the EC2 public interfaces.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000
      6.6. TARGET No Public MCP Backend Port GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000 The EC2 Security Group shall contain no inbound rule for TCP port 8000.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP
      6.8. TARGET Local Proxy Hop GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP The nginx-to-MCP backend hop shall use HTTP only over EC2 loopback at http://127.0.0.1:8000 because public TLS terminates at nginx.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND
      6.5. TARGET MCP Loopback Host Binding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND The MCP container port 8000 shall be published only as 127.0.0.1:8000 on the EC2 host and shall not be bound to the EC2 public interfaces.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
        6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000
    6.6. TARGET No Public MCP Backend Port GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-SG-8000 The EC2 Security Group shall contain no inbound rule for TCP port 8000.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
      6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS
    6.7. TARGET External HTTPS Only GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EXTERNAL-HTTPS Claude and ChatGPT shall reach the MCP service only over HTTPS port 443 through nginx; they shall never connect directly to port 8000 or to an unencrypted public MCP URL.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND
      6.5. TARGET MCP Loopback Host Binding GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-LOOPBACK-BIND The MCP container port 8000 shall be published only as 127.0.0.1:8000 on the EC2 host and shall not be bound to the EC2 public interfaces.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
        6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP
    6.8. TARGET Local Proxy Hop GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INTERNAL-HTTP The nginx-to-MCP backend hop shall use HTTP only over EC2 loopback at http://127.0.0.1:8000 because public TLS terminates at nginx.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
      6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
    6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE
      6.13. TARGET MCP Activation Sequence GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE The independent MCP activation mechanism shall authenticate Docker to ECR through the EC2 instance role, pull the recorded MCP image, remove only a pre-existing MCP container with the recorded container name, and start the replacement MCP container with the approved loopback port publication and runtime configuration.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY
        6.14. TARGET MCP Container Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY The MCP container shall use the Docker restart policy unless-stopped, matching the current Streamlit container's restart behavior.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION
      6.15. TARGET MCP Boot Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION The EC2 host shall use a systemd unit separate from ragstream.service to invoke the independent MCP activation mechanism after network-online.target and docker.service during instance boot.

      The exact unit and activation paths shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before installation.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SSM-INJECTION
      6.16. TARGET MCP Runtime Secret Injection GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SSM-INJECTION The MCP activation mechanism shall read the existing OpenAI API key and every secret required by the selected OAuth realization from recorded SSM Parameter Store references or from the selected external authorization provider at runtime.

      Secret values shall not be embedded in the image, activation artifact, systemd unit, nginx configuration, repository, or local deployment profile.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EC2-ROLE
        6.17. TARGET MCP EC2 Role Permissions GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-EC2-ROLE The existing EC2 instance role shall be extended only as required to pull the recorded MCP image and read the exact SSM parameters used by the MCP activation mechanism.

        The MCP deployment shall not require a static AWS access-key ID or secret access key on EC2.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
      6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-IMAGE-RECORD
    6.10. TARGET MCP Image Reference Recording GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-IMAGE-RECORD The exact image reference used by the MCP container shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before deployment.

    This requirement shall not predetermine whether the MCP implementation uses the current application image or a separate image.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
      6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS
    6.11. TARGET No Project or Memory Data Mounts GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS The initial MCP container shall not mount the current doc_raw, chroma_db, memory, or other project-persistence directories because the approved initial ghost_engineer_prompt tool requires only prompt-processing code and configuration.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE
      10.8. TARGET MCP Scope Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE MCP acceptance shall verify that no document, Chroma, memory, GUI, or other project data mount is attached to the initial MCP container and that no additional GHOST tool is advertised.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
      6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ECR-DELIVERY
    6.12. TARGET Controlled MCP Image Delivery GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ECR-DELIVERY The MCP runtime image shall be built by GitHub Actions and pushed to a private Amazon ECR repository through the same controlled deployment gate used by the current deployment: manual workflow dispatch or an eligible main-branch push whose head commit message contains [deploy].

    The exact Dockerfile, workflow path, ECR repository, and image reference shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before deployment.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
      6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
        6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE
    6.13. TARGET MCP Activation Sequence GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE The independent MCP activation mechanism shall authenticate Docker to ECR through the EC2 instance role, pull the recorded MCP image, remove only a pre-existing MCP container with the recorded container name, and start the replacement MCP container with the approved loopback port publication and runtime configuration.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY
      6.14. TARGET MCP Container Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY The MCP container shall use the Docker restart policy unless-stopped, matching the current Streamlit container's restart behavior.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE
      6.13. TARGET MCP Activation Sequence GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-ACTIVATION-SEQUENCE The independent MCP activation mechanism shall authenticate Docker to ECR through the EC2 instance role, pull the recorded MCP image, remove only a pre-existing MCP container with the recorded container name, and start the replacement MCP container with the approved loopback port publication and runtime configuration.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
        6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
          6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
            6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
            • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
              • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
                1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

                This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY
    6.14. TARGET MCP Container Restart Policy GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESTART-POLICY The MCP container shall use the Docker restart policy unless-stopped, matching the current Streamlit container's restart behavior.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
      6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.

            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.

    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION
    6.15. TARGET MCP Boot Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SYSTEMD-ACTIVATION The EC2 host shall use a systemd unit separate from ragstream.service to invoke the independent MCP activation mechanism after network-online.target and docker.service during instance boot.

    The exact unit and activation paths shall be recorded in the local deployment profile before installation.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION
      6.9. TARGET Independent MCP Activation GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-INDEPENDENT-ACTIVATION The MCP container shall have an activation mechanism independent of the current /usr/local/bin/ragstream-start behavior so MCP deployment or restart does not replace or reconfigure the current Streamlit container.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
        6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
