    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION
    10.2. TARGET Current Streamlit Route Regression Check GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CURRENT-ROUTE-REGRESSION After MCP deployment, https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall continue to serve the current Streamlit application through the existing nginx root route.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
      6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
        6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
        • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
          • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

            This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NETWORK-ACCEPTANCE
    10.3. TARGET MCP Network Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NETWORK-ACCEPTANCE MCP deployment acceptance shall confirm that /mcp is reachable over HTTPS port 443, that host port 8000 listens only on 127.0.0.1, and that the Security Group has no inbound rule for port 8000.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-METADATA
      8.2. TARGET Protected Resource Metadata GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-RESOURCE-METADATA The deployment shall publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata at https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp for the canonical MCP resource https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp and shall identify at least one authorization server used by the clients.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE
        8.1. TARGET OAuth-Protected MCP Resource GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE The MCP endpoint shall act as an OAuth-protected resource server for HTTP transport and shall require a valid access token before processing tool discovery or tool invocation.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
          6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
            6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
            • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
              • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
                1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

                This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
                • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                  2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-DISCOVERY-ACCEPTANCE
    10.4. TARGET OAuth Discovery Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-AUTH-DISCOVERY-ACCEPTANCE MCP deployment acceptance shall confirm that an unauthenticated /mcp request receives the required authorization challenge and that protected-resource and authorization-server discovery documents resolve to the configured canonical values.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE
      8.1. TARGET OAuth-Protected MCP Resource GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE The MCP endpoint shall act as an OAuth-protected resource server for HTTP transport and shall require a valid access token before processing tool discovery or tool invocation.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
        6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE
    10.5. TARGET Claude Client Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CLAUDE-ACCEPTANCE The target shall not be promoted to CURRENT until Claude can add the remote MCP server, complete OAuth authorization, discover ghost_engineer_prompt as the sole GHOST tool, invoke it successfully, receive the post-A2 engineered prompt, and continue after an access-token refresh.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE
      8.1. TARGET OAuth-Protected MCP Resource GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-OAUTH-PROTECTED-RESOURCE The MCP endpoint shall act as an OAuth-protected resource server for HTTP transport and shall require a valid access token before processing tool discovery or tool invocation.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
        6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE
    10.6. TARGET ChatGPT Client Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-CHATGPT-ACCEPTANCE The target shall not be promoted to CURRENT until the owner's ChatGPT web account can create and enable the custom MCP plugin, complete OAuth authorization, discover ghost_engineer_prompt as the sole GHOST tool, invoke it successfully, receive the post-A2 engineered prompt in the same conversation, and continue after an access-token refresh.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP
      7.1. TARGET Streamable HTTP Transport GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-STREAMABLE-HTTP The MCP server shall implement the MCP Streamable HTTP transport at the single /mcp endpoint.

      HTTP POST shall process MCP JSON-RPC messages. HTTP GET shall either open the transport's server-to-client event stream or return HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed when the server does not offer a standalone event stream.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE
        6.4. TARGET Exact nginx MCP Route GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NGINX-ROUTE The Certbot-managed HTTPS server block for ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com shall contain an exact /mcp route that proxies to the local MCP backend without changing the existing Streamlit location / route.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL
          6.3. TARGET MCP Public URL GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PUBLIC-URL The externally configured MCP server URL shall be exactly https://ragstream.rusbehabtahi.com/mcp.
          • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
            6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
            • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
              • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
                1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

                This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
                • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                  2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE
    10.7. TARGET MCP Protocol Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROTOCOL-ACCEPTANCE MCP protocol acceptance shall complete initialize, notifications/initialized, tools/list, and one successful tools/call for ghost_engineer_prompt through the public HTTPS URL.

    The returned result shall contain the engineered prompt produced only after the internal deterministic prompt-construction step and A2 execution have completed.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS
      6.11. TARGET No Project or Memory Data Mounts GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-NO-PROJECT-MOUNTS The initial MCP container shall not mount the current doc_raw, chroma_db, memory, or other project-persistence directories because the approved initial ghost_engineer_prompt tool requires only prompt-processing code and configuration.
      • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER
        6.2. TARGET Separate MCP Container GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SEPARATE-CONTAINER The MCP service shall run in a Docker container separate from the current ragstream Streamlit container.
        • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
          6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
          • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
            1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
            • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
              1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

              This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE
    10.8. TARGET MCP Scope Acceptance GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SCOPE-ACCEPTANCE MCP acceptance shall verify that no document, Chroma, memory, GUI, or other project data mount is attached to the initial MCP container and that no additional GHOST tool is advertised.
    • GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2
      6.1. TARGET MCP on Existing EC2 GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-SAME-EC2 The initial remote MCP service shall run on the same EC2 instance as the current Streamlit deployment.
      • GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY
        1.1. AWS Deployment and Operations Capability GHOST-AWS-DEPLOYMENT-CAPABILITY GHOST shall reproduce the documented current Phase-1 AWS deployment and shall add the target remote MCP deployment without changing the current Streamlit service unless a TARGET requirement explicitly requires an additive change.
        • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY
          1.1. AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY GHOST shall provide the runtime-platform and operations capability required to reproduce the documented current AWS deployment and to add the approved initial remote MCP endpoint.

          This capability shall be limited to AWS hosting, container execution, image delivery, HTTPS routing, DNS, runtime secrets, persistent AWS runtime data, deployment activation, operational verification, and the MCP deployment boundary.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMOTION-GATE
    10.9. TARGET MCP Status Promotion Gate GHOST-AWS-TGT-MCP-PROMOTION-GATE The MCP requirements shall remain TARGET until the implementation exists on the EC2 instance and all network, OAuth discovery, Claude, ChatGPT, protocol, tool-scope, and Streamlit-regression acceptance requirements pass.
